name: Q2146600
abstract: Reunald Jones Sr. (December 22, 1910 – February 26, 1989), was an American jazz trumpeter, who worked in big bands and as a studio musician.

concerts.label: Bel Canto Foundation Jazz Concert to Benefit Musicians

about_quotes.speaker: Vic Dickenson
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Reunald Jones. Do you know Reunald Jones? [INTERVIEWER] Yes.

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: He had Vic Dickenson, he had Roy and Joe Eldridge, Gus Wilson and Teddy Wilson. And Eli Robinson and Reunald Jones, who we called Jonesy. So that was quite a band.

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Clark
about_quotes.evidence: Reunald Jones, for instance, with the Basie band, used to sit there all night and play with one hand. Well Ham Davis did that before he was born, just about.
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Clark — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Freddie Green
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...you have Paul Campbell, and Wendell Culley, Reunald Jones, Joe Newman, Henry Coker, Benny Powell, Jimmy Wilkins. [INTERVIEWEE] Mm hmm.

about_quotes.speaker: Teddy Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: Reunald Jones from the Atlanta band; Reunald Jones finally wound up, some years ago, he was lead trumpet fo Basie, back about 20 years ago.
